name: Deacon
description:
Library component. Models the Deacon session that runs `bd gate check` and `gt gate wake`. The two-step protocol -- close gate, then send wake -- is non-atomic; if the Deacon session dies between the two operations the wake is never sent and parked polecats wait indefinitely. See gastown #3587.

Flow:
  Idle -> ClosingGate (send Close to gate)
  ClosingGate -> WakeReady (observe Gate=Closed, i.e. the close was processed)
  WakeReady -> Done (send Wake to polecat)

The critical bug-window transition:
  WakeReady -> Crashed (receive Kill on KILL_IN -- Deacon session dies after gate-close has landed but before wake-send fires)

v0.2 (defense-doc P1.1): the crash is now triggered by an external Kill message on KILL_IN (typically wired from a Reaper component) rather than an actor-internal nondeterministic transition. This makes the crash semantic explicit -- the death is caused by an external SIGTERM/OOM/pane-close event, not by Deacon 'choosing' to crash.

TLC explores both branches from WakeReady (receive Kill -> Crashed, or send Wake -> Done) when Kill is in flight. The Crashed branch leaves the gate Closed, no wake in flight, Polecat permanently Parked -- the bug shape #3587 reports.

Real sources: cmd/bd/gate.go:818 closeGate(), internal/cmd/gate.go:84 gt gate wake.

GATE_CLOSE_OUT.description: send Close to the gate (bd gate check determined the dependency resolved). Tagged 'Close'.

GATE_OBSERVE.description: observe the gate's state. Used both to gate Idle -> ClosingGate (only act on Open gates -- mirrors `bd gate check` only querying status=open) AND to confirm the close landed before proceeding to send wake.

POLECAT_OBSERVE.description: observe the polecat's lifecycle state. v0.3: Idle -> ClosingGate is gated on observing polecat=Parked, mirroring real-world causality where the polecat has long since parked by the time Deacon's scheduled `bd gate check` runs. Without this gate, TLC could explore interleavings where Deacon races ahead of the WorkLoop's parking trigger -- producing a spurious counterexample that doesn't exist in real gastown.

WAKE_OUT.description: send Wake to the parked polecat (gt gate wake). Tagged 'Wake'. The bug arises when this send is preempted by Deacon session death.

KILL_IN.description: receives Kill from an external Reaper, modeling SIGTERM/OOM/pane-close. Tagged 'Kill'. Only consumed when Deacon is in WakeReady (the crash-window state); Kill arriving in other states sits in flight (no triple matches), modeling 'death only matters during the protocol's vulnerable window for this bug demonstration'.

actors.name: Deacon
actors.owner: Deacon session running bd gate check + gt gate wake. Non-atomic two-step protocol with explicit crash window between the two sends.

triples.event: bd gate check finds polecat parked on an open gate; close it
gate close landed; ready to send wake
gt gate wake sends Wake to parked polecat
BUG WINDOW: external Kill received after closing gate but before sending wake
terminal: session dead, no further protocol progress
